title: DAPI-Based Polyphosphate Estimation with Extraction Sufficiency Validation: A Method for Quantifying Polyphosphate from Microalgae Samples
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"8tgse","text":"The utilization of DAPI-based fluorometric estimation for polyphosphate (polyP) analysis from microalgae has become increasingly prevalent in field samples since its publication by Martin P. et al. This technique involves evaluating the fluorescence of DAPI-stained samples in quartz cuvettes using a spectrofluorometer. To reduce the consumption of reagent, time, and labor while minimizing DAPI photobleaching, we have adapted this method to a 96-well black microtiter plate with a black film-covered lid. Additionally, the calculation method has been modified to account for matrix effects in microplates.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3hnj6","text":"Testing the number of treatment rounds necessary to extract all polyP is crucial. However, even when collecting samples from the same field location or cultivation condition, there can be high variability in treatment rounds among replicates, leading to significant background fluorescence and rendering the polyP from the sample undetectable. This challenge is especially prominent when measuring polyP from field samples. Limited sample availability and insufficient polyP extraction, combined with high background fluorescence, make the laborious measurement unpredictable and hinder accurate polyP measurement. This obstacle is a significant hurdle in polyP measurement.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6c5f9","text":"In our assay, we overcome the challenge by validating the sufficiency of extraction for each sample and then measuring the polyP values. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d7dk","text":"To conduct the assay, roughly 400 uL RNase, 400 uL DNase, and 700 uL proteinase are required for four samples.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4l4qj","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"6g7a5","text":"","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8cilh","text":"","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"citation","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"495E48D0341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","title":"Fluorometric Quantification of Polyphosphate in Environmental Plankton Samples: Extraction Protocols, Matrix Effects, and Nucleic Acid Interference","protocol_guid":"","journal":"Applied and Environmental Microbiology","date":null,"created_on":"2026-02-10T22:33:37Z","doi":"http://doi.org/10.1128/AEM.02592-12","authors":"Martin, Patrick \u0026 Van Mooy, Benjamin","can_edit":false}}}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"fc0et","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bfhna","text":"Extracted polyphosphate must be measured on the same day. Polyphosphate loss has been observed if the extraction is processed days after.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dv2n","text":"The polyphosphate standard aliquot can only be thawed and used once. Do not refrozen and thawed multiple times.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6s24g","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Filter microalgae in liquid media onto GFF or PC filters, using gentle vacuum pressure (5 inches Hg). Filter forceps (blunt end, stainless steel, Millipore, XX6200006P)
Rinse sample with filtered artificial seawater (no nutrients)
Place sample filters in cryogenic vials
Filter blank media (without cells) through GFF or PC filter as blank.
Flash freeze filters and stored at -20 °C
Freeze dry before measurement. FreeZone® 2.5 L Benchtop Freeze Dryers (Labconco®, 700202000)
Tris buffer 20 mM pH 7.0 Budget: About 250 mL per four samples
In a 1 L volumetric flask, top 20 mL 1 M pH 7.0 Tris buffer to 1 L with MilliQ
Store at Room temperature
PolyP primary standard stock
Weigh one glass pellet of polyP (45) and write down the weight. Microbalance (Cubis series, Sartorius, MSE6.6S-000-DM)
Transfer the pellet into a 100 mL graduated cylinder.
Dilute to 100 mL with Tris 20 mM pH 7.0
Aliquot primary stock into 10~50 uL per microtube with Stepper and store at -20 °C
PolyP secondary standard stock If the pellet is far more than 10 mg, dilute primary to secondary to bring down the concentration before preparing working standard
Proteinase K 20 mg/ml
Add 25 mL MilliQ directly into the original package of Proteinase K, vortex to mix
Aliquot 700 uL to microtubes and keep frozen at -20 °C
DAPI primary stock 14.3 mM Add 2 mL MilliQ directly into the original package and keep frozen at -20 °C
Prepare boiling bath. VWR® Advanced Hot Plates (VWR, 97042-658) Hollow Polypropylene (PP) Ball Bath Covers, 20 mm (Cole-Parmer, UZ-06821-04) Tube rack (Simport MultiRack™, CA48648-606)
Transfer sample into glass centrifuge tube Disposable Glass Screw-Cap Centrifuge Tubes (10 mL, Corning®, 99502-10)
Add 4 mL Tris buffer 20 mM pH 7.0 , vortex and then sonicate. Specific Pipette Tips 5mL (Thermo Scientific™ Finntip™, 21-377-304)
Keep in boiling bath. Make sure the tube rack is in the middle of the boiling bath and covered with PP balls. Tris solution in the tube should be boiling during the 5 minutes' incubation.
Sonicate

Vortex and then transfer extract to 15 mL Falcon tube, according to the extract number. Do not push filter to the bottom. Use Pasteur pipet, gently lift the filter upwards, and then transfer as much extract as possible. Gently press the extract out of the filter. Disposable Soda-Lime Glass Pasteur Pipets (5 3/4", Fisherbrand, 13-678-6A)
Repeat Step 17 to Step 20 until complete 15 times' extraction in total.

Use forward pipetting, load black microtitre plate with 200 µL supernatant from the extract (one well for one extract, no need to load replicates). Tris buffer 20 mM pH 7.0 is used as blank. 96-Well Black Microplates (Polystyrene, Greiner Bio-One, 655076)
Prepare DAPI working solution 100 uM Dilute 12.6 µL of 14.3 mM DAPI stock with 1800 µL MilliQ in a foil wrapped microtube and vortex.
In the dimmed room with only red light bulb on add 24 µL 100 uM DAPI to each sample in the plate.
Adhere black film on the top of a microplate lid and cover the plate with this lid. Black Vinyl Films for Fluorescence and Photoprotection (VWR, 89087-692)
Shake at room temperature for 00:07:00
Read fluorescence: excitation at 410 nm and emission at 550 nm Varioskan LUX Multimode Microplate Reader (Thermo Fisher, VL0L00D0)

Plot fluorescence intensity versus number of extraction. The number of extract (N) is the stationary point where the fluorescence of stained extract stops decreasing or the derivative of the fluorescence after that point is close to zero. If RFU(15)-RFU(Tris)>1 , proceed to extract five additional times. And then measure the stained extract following the previous steps.
Well mix 1~N extract, transfer 12 mL into 15 mL falcon tube, centrifuge 3200 rpm, Room temperature, 5 minutes

In Set S, add 18 µL RNase and 18 µL DNase RNase tends to leave residue in the tip. However one package has only 1 mL RNase, it will be a waste to use reverse pipetting. After dispensing RNase into the vial, use the same tip to draw the solution and gently dispense it back into the solution for about three time, so that there is no residue remaining in the tip. Replace a new tip for the next vial. Require ~400 uL RNase and ~400 uL DNase.
Incubate at 37 °C , shake continuously SHAKING INCUBATOR (71L, Corning® LSE™, 6753) Start the timer when temperature reaches 37 °C
In Set S, add 36 µL Proteinase
Incubate at 37 °C , shake continuously. Start the timer when temperature reaches 37 °C
Prepare DAPI working solution 100 uM Dilute 12.6 µL of 14.3 mM DAPI stock with 1800 µL MilliQ in a foil wrapped microtube and vortex.

Prepare PolyP working standard [PO3]~ 7.6 uM Based on the actual concentration of PolyP (45) primary or secondary standard stock, dilute a certain volume of stock with Tris buffer 20 mM pH 7.0 For a final concentration 7.6 uM Total volume = 160 X N (ul), where N = sample number FW(45Na2O.55P2O5)=10600 Mol of PO3 per mol of PolyP (45) = 110
Transfer 840 µL of enzyme treated extract (1~N) into 2 mL tubes (Set A). Forward pipetting, aspire and dispense for three times to mix.
Add 160 µL 7.6 uM polyP working standard to 840 µL of enzyme treated extract, vortex.
Load 200 µL blanks (B: N+1), samples (S: 1~N) and amended samples (A: Amended 1~N) to the microplate. Organize samples as shown in the following scheme: Reverse pipetting
In a dimmed room with only red bulb on, add 24 µL DAPI working solution 100 uM to each sample in the microplate except for those labelled with (UN) . Forward pipetting
Adhere black film on the top of a microplate lid and cover the plate with this lid.
Shake at room temperature for 00:07:00
Shake duration: 1 min Shaking type: continuous Shaking speed and force: 600 rpm/High Fluorescence: excitation at 410 nm and emission at 550 nm Measurement time: 300 ms Excitation bandwidth: 5 nm
Centrifuge the extract 3200 rpm, Room temperature, 5 minutes
Centrifuge extract "N+1" 3200 rpm, Room temperature, 5 minutes Blank is duplicated into B1a and B1b; B2a and B2b... etc.
Transfer 1.8 mL supernatant to a 2 mL tube (Set S). Sample is triplicated into S1a, S1b and S1c; S2a, S2b, S2c...etc.
Transfer 1.5 mL supernatant into a 2 mL tube (Set B).
In Set B, add 15 µL RNase and 15 µL DNase
Thaw proteinase (~700uL)
In Set B, add 30 µL Proteinase

Combine Extraction 1 to Extraction N into a falcon tube. Try to transfer all solution including debris from each tube. If the total volume is over 50 mL, use a beaker instead. Sample code N V(Tris) per extract (mL) 1 2 3 4
Label centrifuge tube for different samples, place one Pasteur pipet into the tube for transferring extract from the same sample
Label 15 mL Falcon tube from 1 to 15 for each one sample.
Prepare 37 °C incubator/shaker.
creator: Yingyu Hu
